subject: Cloud Computing
title: How Your Data Reaches the Cloud
description: Learn How Your Data Reaches the Cloud in one minute.

hook.text: You click 'save' and your file appears in the cloud. But it actually traveled thousands of miles.

buildup.text: Data leaves your device, hits your router, travels to your ISP, then crosses fiber-optic backbone networks.

discovery.text: Cloud providers build private backbone networks connecting their data centers at the speed of light.

twist.text: Most of your internet traffic never touches the 'public' internet. It rides on private fiber.

climax.text: Google alone operates over 100,000 miles of private undersea and land fiber-optic cable.

punchline.text: The cloud feels instant. The fiber underneath makes it so.

quiz.question: How do major cloud providers connect their global data centers?
options: Through private fiber-optic backbone networks they own and operate
Exclusively through the public internet like everyone else
Using satellite connections for all inter-datacenter traffic
